tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows hypoglycemia and markedly low HbA1c, suggesting chronic low blood glucose. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though diabetes control appears excessive. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for hypoglycemia, renal impairment, and further nephrology/endocrinology evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
